title: measureCombinedGitOutputBytes can return an underestimate when a middle arg set errors with ENOBUFS
body: In lib/git.mjs, measureGitOutputBytes treats an ENOBUFS error as 'output is at least maxBytes + 1' and returns that sentinel, but measureCombinedGitOutputBytes accumulates totalBytes across multiple arg sets and only checks `remainingBytes < 0` before each call -- if an early arg set hits ENOBUFS the loop still continues to the next arg set with a negative remainingBytes budget, calling measureGitOutputBytes with maxBytes+1 as a negative number.

title: getJobTypeLabel priority order makes jobClass 'task' always show as 'rescue' even for adversarial-review jobs
body: job-control.mjs's getJobTypeLabel checks job.kind === 'adversarial-review' first, but then checks job.jobClass === 'task' (-> 'rescue') before falling through to job.kind === 'review'/'task'. If a job has both kind: 'review' and jobClass: 'task' set (which happens for review jobs spawned as rescue tasks), it's mislabeled as 'rescue' in the status UI instead of 'review'.

title: filterJobsForCurrentSession silently returns all jobs when SESSION_ID_ENV is unset
body: getCurrentSessionId() in job-control.mjs falls back through options.env, process.env, then null; when sessionId is null/falsy, filterJobsForCurrentSession just returns every job unfiltered. In a shared job state directory this means /status can leak jobs from other Claude sessions if the env var isn't propagated (e.g. a hook run through a sandboxed shell).

title: readJobProgressPreview drops lines that legitimately start with '[' but aren't log-prefixed
body: readJobProgressPreview filters lines with .filter((line) => line.startsWith("[")) assuming every real log line has a bracketed timestamp/tag prefix, but a model's actual output line that happens to start with a literal '[' (e.g. quoting markdown like '[link](url)') gets silently treated as a log line and stripped via stripLogPrefix's regex, corrupting the preview text shown to the user.

title: Document the broker-endpoint lifecycle for app-server-broker.mjs
body: plugins/codex/scripts/app-server-broker.mjs and lib/broker-endpoint.mjs / lib/broker-lifecycle.mjs implement a broker process that other scripts (codex-companion.mjs) talk to, but there's no doc describing what happens if the broker crashes mid-job -- do tracked jobs in lib/tracked-jobs.mjs get orphaned or resumed on next broker start?
